title: Add and Subtract Decimals

projects.title: Unit 1 Project — Number System in Action
projects.desc: Apply factors, decimals, and division to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 1.

projects.title: Cooking Simulator — Decimal Operations
projects.desc: Add and subtract decimals to complete recipes in this cooking simulator (6.NS.3).

projects.title: Decimal Docking Bay (Part 1)
projects.desc: Interactive graphic novel: add and subtract decimals to dock the ship (6.NS.3).

contentObjective: I can add and subtract decimals by lining up the place values and the decimal points.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words decimal, place value, tenths, hundredths, and annex zeros.

turnAndTalk.question: The fuel tank reads 128.75 liters, a shuttle delivers 46.8 liters, and docking thrusters used 19.35 liters. Why must you line up the decimal points before adding or subtracting these amounts?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Lining up the decimal points keeps each place value matched.

stems.en: I line up the ___ so tenths add to tenths and hundredths add to hundredths.
stems.es: Alineo los ___ para que los décimos se sumen con décimos y los centésimos con centésimos.

stems.en: If the points are not aligned, then ___.
stems.es: Si los puntos no están alineados, entonces ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain that aligning decimal points lines up matching place values (tenths under tenths, hundredths under hundredths) so the addition and subtraction are correct.
turnAndTalk.extend: Estimate the final fuel level by rounding each number, then explain how that estimate checks your exact answer of 128.75 + 46.8 − 19.35.
extendStems: I estimate about ___ liters because I round to ___.
My exact answer is reasonable because it is close to ___.

turnAndTalk.question: When you added 128.75 and 46.8, how did you handle 46.8 having fewer decimal places, and when did you need to regroup?
turnAndTalk.kernel: You can annex a zero so 46.8 becomes 46.80 and every place value lines up.

stems.en: I annexed a zero to write 46.8 as ___ so the place values matched.
stems.es: Agregué un cero para escribir 46.8 como ___ para que los valores posicionales coincidieran.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students annex a zero to write 46.80, aligning hundredths, and explain regrouping happens when a column sum is 10 or more (or borrowing is needed when subtracting).
turnAndTalk.extend: Why does annexing a zero to make 46.8 into 46.80 not change its value? Justify with place value.
extendStems: Annexing a zero does not change the value because ___.
The extra zero is in the ___ place, which means ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A runner's three laps are 2.45, 2.6, and 2.38 minutes. How do you add decimals with different numbers of places to find her total time?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Annex zeros so all times have the same places, then line up and add.

stems.en: Her total time is ___ minutes because I added ___, ___, and ___.
stems.es: Su tiempo total es ___ minutos porque sumé ___, ___ y ___.

stems.en: I wrote 2.6 as ___ so every place value lined up.
stems.es: Escribí 2.6 como ___ para que cada valor posicional se alineara.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students annex zeros (2.60), align the decimal points, and add 2.45 + 2.60 + 2.38 = 7.43 minutes.
turnAndTalk.extend: Her personal record is 7.5 minutes. Did she beat it? Compare 7.43 and 7.5 using place value and justify.
extendStems: She ___ beat her record because 7.43 is ___ than 7.5.

turnAndTalk.question: Why does lining up place values matter MORE with decimals than with whole numbers, where you can just line up the right edge?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Decimals have different lengths, so only the decimal point guarantees matched places.

stems.en: With decimals I must line up the ___, not just the right edge, because ___.
stems.es: Con decimales debo alinear los ___, no solo el borde derecho, porque ___.

stems.en: If I lined up the right edge, then ___.
stems.es: Si alineara el borde derecho, entonces ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain that decimals can have different numbers of places, so lining up the decimal point (not the right edge) is what keeps tenths with tenths and hundredths with hundredths.
turnAndTalk.extend: Create an addition where lining up the right edge instead of the decimal point gives a wrong answer, and show the correct sum.
extendStems: If I lined up 3.5 and 0.42 by the right edge, I would wrongly get ___.

vocabulary.definition: A number with a dot that shows a part between whole numbers.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número con un punto que muestra una parte entre números enteros.
vocabulary.visual: 3.75 means 3 ones + 7 tenths + 5 hundredths — it is between 3 and 4
vocabulary.cloze: A number that uses a point to show a value less than one whole is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: What a digit is worth based on where it sits in a number.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Lo que vale un dígito según el lugar que ocupa en el número.
vocabulary.visual: In 4.38: the 4 = 4 ones, the 3 = 3 tenths (0.3), the 8 = 8 hundredths (0.08)
vocabulary.cloze: The value a digit has based on its position in a number is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: The first spot after the decimal point. It shows parts out of 10.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El primer lugar después del punto decimal. Muestra partes de 10.
vocabulary.visual: 0.7 = 7/10 — like 7 slices of a pizza cut into 10 equal slices
vocabulary.cloze: The first place to the right of the decimal point is the ___ place.

examples.why: That 3 is in the hundredths place.

vocabulary.definition: The second spot after the decimal point. It shows parts out of 100.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El segundo lugar después del punto decimal. Muestra partes de 100.
vocabulary.visual: 0.05 = 5/100 — like 5 pennies out of a dollar (100 pennies)
vocabulary.cloze: The second place to the right of the decimal point is the ___ place.

examples.why: That 7 is in the tenths place.

vocabulary.definition: Adding zeros at the end of a decimal so numbers line up evenly.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Agregar ceros al final de un decimal para que los números se alineen.
vocabulary.visual: 15.7 becomes 15.70 so you can subtract 15.70 − 8.36 with aligned place values
vocabulary.cloze: Writing extra zeros at the end of a decimal so the places line up is called ___.

launch.narrative: The space station's main fuel tank reads 128.75 liters. A resupply shuttle delivers 46.8 liters of fuel, but the thrusters used 19.35 liters during docking. What is the fuel level after both events?
launch.contextImage: Space station fuel gauge display showing digital decimal readings with a shuttle docking nearby
noticePrompts: What is the starting fuel amount?
Which operation adds fuel and which removes fuel?
wonderPrompts: Why is it important to line up the decimal points?
How do you handle adding 46.8 (one decimal place) to 128.75 (two decimal places)?

visual.title: Fuel added vs. fuel used
visual.caption: Line up the decimal points so tenths add to tenths and hundredths to hundredths

explore.instructions: Plot each decimal on the number line, then find the sum or difference as indicated.

discourse.prompt: How does a number line help you see what happens when you add or subtract decimals?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When I add ___ to ___, I move ___ to the right on the number line and land on ___.

optional.stem: Maya buys items for $8.75 and $3.40. She pays with $20. How much change does she get?

approaching.explanation: Line up decimals: 14.60 + 3.85. Hundredths: 0 + 5 = 5. Tenths: 6 + 8 = 14, write 4 carry 1. Ones: 4 + 3 + 1 = 8. Tens: 1. Answer: 18.45.
Rewrite as 20.30 − 7.48. Annex a zero so both have two decimal places. Work carefully with borrowing: 20.30 − 7.48 = 12.82.
Line up decimals: 9.05 + 4.70. Hundredths: 5 + 0 = 5. Tenths: 0 + 7 = 7. Ones: 9 + 4 = 13. Answer: 13.75.

approaching.instructions: Place the result of each operation on the number line.
approaching.label: Show 6.5 − 2.8 and 3.25 + 1.5 on the number line

approaching.explanation: Rewrite 50 as 50.00. Then 50.00 − 23.47 = 26.53. You must borrow across multiple places.

onLevel.label: Compute each decimal sum or difference.

onLevel.instructions: Estimate each sum or difference by rounding to the nearest whole number, then sort: is the result greater than 10 or less than 10?

onLevel.stem: A student buys items costing $4.59, $12.75, and $3.80. What is the total cost?
onLevel.explanation: 4.59 + 12.75 + 3.80 = 21.14. Line up decimal points and add column by column.

onLevel.label: Match each expression to its answer.

extending.title: Find Tasha's Mistake
extending.correctWork: Tasha did not write 15.7 as 15.70 before subtracting. With proper alignment: 15.70 − 8.36 = 7.34. She got 7.41 because she subtracted hundredths incorrectly without the placeholder zero.
hints: Look at how Tasha set up the subtraction. Does 15.7 have the same number of decimal places as 8.36?
Rewrite 15.7 as 15.70, then subtract. Hundredths: 0 − 6 requires borrowing.

extending.prompt: A runner's lap times are 2.45 minutes, 2.6 minutes, and 2.38 minutes. Her personal record is 7.5 minutes total. Did she beat her record? Show all your work with decimal alignment and explain.
extending.sentenceFrame: First I lined up the decimals: 2.45 + 2.60 + 2.38 = ___. Compared to 7.50, the runner ___ beat her record because ___.

extending.label: Find the missing number in each equation.

connect.scenario: A runner's times for three laps are 2.45 minutes, 2.6 minutes, and 2.38 minutes. She wants to find her total time to see if she beat her personal record of 7.5 minutes.
connect.promptQuestion: How do you add decimals with different numbers of decimal places to find her total time?
connect.prompt: The runner's total time is ___ minutes because 2.45 + 2.60 + 2.38 = ___.

exitTicket.explanation: Rewrite as 32.60 − 14.85. Hundredths: 0 − 5 → borrow → 10 − 5 = 5. Tenths: 5 − 8 → borrow → 15 − 8 = 7. Ones: 1 − 4 → borrow → 11 − 4 = 7. Tens: 2 − 1 = 1. Answer: 17.75.
